proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib.

analyses.result_summary: ORR is 0.1739 with treatment_pembrolizumab=1 vs 0.1640 with treatment_pembrolizumab=0; absolute difference +0.0099 (chi-square p=3.4e-03). Small but statistically significant overall benefit.
ORR is 0.1695 with sotorasib vs 0.1687 without; difference +0.0008 (p=0.83). No detectable overall benefit unconditional on biomarker.
ORR is 0.1699 with olaparib vs 0.1685 without; difference +0.0013 (p=0.72). No detectable overall benefit.
ORR is 0.1655 with osimertinib vs 0.1704 without; difference -0.0050 (p=0.18). Numerically lower with treatment, not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within egfr_mutation=1 patients, those receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
Within kras_g12c=1 patients, those receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, those receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
Within tmb_high=1 patients, those receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.

analyses.code: sub=df[df['egfr_mutation']==1]; chi2_contingency on treatment_osimertinib x objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Among 6517 EGFR-mutant patients, ORR is 0.1637 on osimertinib vs 0.1657 off (diff -0.0020, p=0.87). No biomarker-matched benefit.

analyses.code: sub=df[df['kras_g12c']==1]; chi2_contingency on treatment_sotorasib x objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Among 6368 KRAS G12C-positive patients, ORR is 0.1745 on sotorasib vs 0.1664 off (diff +0.0081, p=0.43). No matched benefit.

analyses.code: sub=df[df['brca2_mutation']==1]; chi2_contingency on treatment_olaparib x objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Among 1519 BRCA2-mutant patients, ORR is 0.1463 on olaparib vs 0.1679 off (diff -0.0216, p=0.35). No detectable benefit; trend numerically the wrong direction.

analyses.code: sub=df[df['tmb_high']==1]; chi2_contingency on treatment_pembrolizumab x objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Among 14258 TMB-high patients, ORR is 0.1967 on pembrolizumab vs 0.1672 off (diff +0.0295, p=5.4e-06). Robust ORR benefit in the TMB-high stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Patients with stage_iv=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with stage_iv=0.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with has_brain_mets=0.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with liver_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by ECOG: 0=0.211, 1=0.157, 2=0.111. Logistic beta=-0.375 per unit (p=1.7e-93). Strong negative association.
ORR=0.196 in stage_iv=0 vs 0.154 in stage_iv=1. Logit beta=-0.295 (p=1.3e-33). Stage IV strongly lowers response.
ORR=0.178 vs 0.144 with brain mets. Logit beta=-0.252 (p=2.0e-18). Significant negative association.
ORR=0.168 vs 0.173 with liver mets. Logit beta=+0.032 (p=0.33). No effect; bone, adrenal, pleural, pericardial mets also null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta=+0.091 per g/dL (p=1.4e-04). Higher albumin associates with higher ORR, as expected.
Logit beta=-0.00487 per mg/L (p=6.9e-04). Modest but significant negative association.
Logit beta=-0.0385 per percentage-point loss (p=1.8e-32). Strong negative association of weight loss with ORR.
Logit beta=+0.166 per fractional unit pdl1_tps (p=3.2e-03). Marginal univariate signal; strongest signal will come from interaction with pembrolizumab (see iter 5).
Logit beta=-0.0050 per unit (p=0.38). Univariate not significant; sign is negative as expected.
Logit beta=+0.0001 per U/L (p=0.51). No univariate signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction such that the increase in objective_response associated with treatment_pembrolizumab is larger when pdl1_tps is higher.
There is a positive interaction such that the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is larger when tmb_high=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.591 (p=1.6e-07). PD-L1 TPS strongly amplifies pembrolizumab benefit. Pembro main effect (at pdl1_tps=0) beta=-0.114 (p=0.007); pembro is harmful or null when PD-L1 is zero and beneficial at high PD-L1.
Interaction beta = +0.184 (p=4.1e-04). TMB-high amplifies pembrolizumab benefit. Pembro main effect (at tmb_high=0) beta=+0.015 (p=0.60).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction: stk11_mutation=1 attenuates the objective_response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab.
There is a negative interaction: keap1_mutation=1 attenuates the objective_response benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.211 (p=1.8e-03). STK11 mutation significantly attenuates pembrolizumab benefit, consistent with published immunotherapy resistance literature in NSCLC.
Interaction beta = -0.058 (p=0.36). No significant interaction between KEAP1 mutation and pembrolizumab.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction such that treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response more when egfr_mutation=1.
There is a positive interaction such that treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response more when kras_g12c=1.
There is a positive interaction such that treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more when brca2_mutation=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.024 (p=0.76). No detectable EGFR-driven amplification of osimertinib effect in this dataset.
Interaction beta = +0.059 (p=0.43). No KRAS G12C-driven amplification of sotorasib effect.
Interaction beta = -0.178 (p=0.27). Sign is opposite to expected; not significant. None of the three classical genotype-matched targeted-therapy interactions are detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a higher objective_response rate than male patients.
Objective_response differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Objective_response differs across insurance_type categories.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have a different objective_response rate than urban patients.

analyses.result_summary: ORR: female 0.1754 vs male 0.1637 (diff +0.0117, p=5.9e-04). Modest but statistically significant female advantage.
ORR: white 0.1655, hispanic 0.1715, black 0.1776, asian 0.1772, other 0.1854. Overall chi-square p=4.5e-02. Marginal heterogeneity; white slightly lowest, but small absolute spread.
ORR: private 0.1720, medicare 0.1685, medicaid 0.1613, uninsured 0.1700. Chi-square p=0.23. No significant effect of insurance.
ORR: urban 0.1695 vs rural 0.1666 (diff -0.0029, p=0.50). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta=-0.0222 per grade (p=0.040). Marginally significant negative association.
Logit beta=+0.0013 per unit (p=0.78). No association.
Logit beta=-0.0074 per grade (p=0.49). No association.
Logit beta=-0.0104 per grade (p=0.35). No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: copd=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
interstitial_lung_disease_history=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
autoimmune_disease=1 is associated with different objective_response than autoimmune_disease=0.
diabetes_mellitus=1 is associated with different objective_response.
coronary_artery_disease=1 is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta=-0.024 (p=0.37). No detectable effect.
Logit beta=-0.071 (p=0.26). No detectable effect.
Logit beta=+0.046 (p=0.40). No effect.
Logit beta=+0.0093 (p=0.74). No effect.
Logit beta=-0.062 (p=0.049). Borderline significant negative association; survives only at unadjusted p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_chemotherapy=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
prior_immunotherapy=1 is associated with different objective_response.
prior_targeted_therapy=1 is associated with different objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta=-0.044 (p=0.065). Borderline negative association.
Logit beta=-0.012 per line (p=0.24). No detectable effect.
Logit beta=+0.030 (p=0.38). No effect.
Logit beta=+0.038 (p=0.30). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 23 single-nucleotide polymorphisms in the panel (snp_rs*), at least one is associated with objective_response at unadjusted p<0.05.
After Bonferroni correction across 23 SNPs, at least one SNP remains significantly associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: for snp in snp_cols: Logit(objective_response ~ snp)
analyses.result_summary: Two SNPs reached unadjusted p<0.05: snp_rs7412 (beta=+0.100, p=0.018) and snp_rs1050828 (beta=-0.099, p=0.031). The other 21 SNPs are non-significant.
Neither snp_rs7412 (p=0.018) nor snp_rs1050828 (p=0.031) survives Bonferroni at alpha=0.00217. Consistent with multiple-testing noise; no SNP-response association withstands correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
msi_high=1 is associated with higher objective_response.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with different objective_response.
her2_amplification=1 is associated with different objective_response.

Among the panel of additional alterations (cdkn2a_loss, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, met_exon14_skipping, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, alk_fusion), none has a univariate association with objective_response at unadjusted p<0.05.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta=-0.0038 (p=0.87). No association.
Logit beta=-0.049 (p=0.69). No detectable association (n+ = 487).
Logit beta=+0.133 (p=0.106). Numerically positive but not significant.
Logit beta=-0.050 (p=0.49). No association.
All ten univariate p-values exceed 0.05 (smallest p=0.09 for pik3ca). Hypothesis of no detectable univariate associations is supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within PD-L1 high (pdl1_tps>=0.5) patients, treatment_pembrolizumab markedly increases objective_response compared to no pembrolizumab.
Within PD-L1 negative (pdl1_tps==0) patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not increase objective_response.
Within PD-L1 low-positive (1-49%) patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not increase objective_response.

analyses.code: sub=df[pdl1_tps>=0.5]; chi2_contingency on pembro x ORR
analyses.result_summary: Among 11412 PD-L1 high patients, ORR is 0.207 on pembrolizumab vs 0.155 off (diff +0.0516, p=9.3e-13). Strong, highly significant pembrolizumab benefit confined to the high-PD-L1 stratum.
Among 5791 PD-L1 negative patients, ORR is 0.170 on vs 0.172 off (diff -0.0023, p=0.85). Confirms no benefit when PD-L1 is zero.
Among 32797 PD-L1 low-positive patients, ORR is 0.163 on vs 0.166 off (diff -0.0025, p=0.56). Confirms benefit is restricted to the high (>=50%) stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint biomarker subgroup defined by pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab produces a substantially larger absolute increase in objective_response than in either marker alone.

analyses.result_summary: Among 3150 patients with PD-L1>=50% AND TMB-high, ORR is 0.294 on pembrolizumab vs 0.155 off (absolute diff +0.139, p=1.9e-20). Joint biomarker selection roughly triples the absolute ORR gain seen in either marker alone, consistent with a positive multiplicative interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction such that the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is greater in female patients than in male patients.
Treatment_pembrolizumab does not benefit never-smokers but benefits former and current smokers.
There is a negative interaction such that the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is smaller in EGFR-mutant patients (egfr_mutation=1).
There is a negative interaction such that ecog_ps reduces the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.146 (p=2.4e-03). Female patients derive a significantly larger pembrolizumab benefit than males in this dataset.

Pembro effect on ORR: current +0.0124 (p=0.048), former +0.0108 (p=0.017), never +0.0012 (p=0.91). Benefit is concentrated among ever-smokers; never-smokers show no benefit, consistent with the lower TMB seen in never-smoker NSCLC.

analyses.code: sub=df[egfr_mutation==1]; chi2 on pembro x ORR
analyses.result_summary: Among 6517 EGFR-mutant patients, pembro on=0.168 vs off=0.162 (diff +0.0055, p=0.57). No detectable benefit, consistent with attenuated immunotherapy response in EGFR-driven NSCLC.
Interaction beta = -0.036 (p=0.32). No significant ECOG x pembrolizumab interaction; pembrolizumab benefit appears similar across ECOG strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction such that has_brain_mets attenuates pembrolizumab benefit.
There is a negative interaction such that NLR x CRP (combined inflammation) is associated with lower objective_response over and above each main effect.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.033 (p=0.57). No detectable brain-mets x pembrolizumab interaction; pembrolizumab benefit is similar with and without brain mets.

After adjusting for main effects, the NLR x CRP interaction beta = +0.00186 (p=2.4e-03). With main effects nlr beta=-0.0162 (p=0.019) and crp beta=-0.0117 (p=2.2e-05), the joint inflammation effect is non-additive: at very high values of one marker the additional negative impact of the other diminishes (positive product term).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model that simultaneously adjusts for age, sex, histology, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, keap1_mutation, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, albumin_g_dl, crp_mg_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, the four treatments, and the pembro x pdl1_tps, pembro x tmb_high, and pembro x stk11 interactions, the pembrolizumab interaction terms remain significant predictors of objective_response.
In the same multivariable model, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, crp_mg_l, and weight_loss_pct_6mo remain independent prognostic predictors of objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ X) with all listed terms
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted estimates: pembro x pdl1_tps beta=+0.595 (p=1.7e-07); pembro x tmb_high beta=+0.173 (p=9.9e-04); pembro x stk11 beta=-0.204 (p=2.8e-03). All three pembrolizumab interactions remain robust after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Same model; check coefficients on baseline prognostic features
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: ecog_ps beta=-0.382 (p<1e-90); stage_iv beta=-0.302 (p<1e-30); has_brain_mets beta=-0.258 (p<1e-15); albumin_g_dl beta=+0.092 (p=1.2e-04); crp_mg_l beta=-0.0051 (p=4.5e-04); weight_loss_pct_6mo beta=-0.039 (p<1e-30); sex_female beta=+0.080 (p=8.7e-04). All retain independence.
